description: CorpusStudio context-source registry: the loop's document-mode/authority map. Hand-authored from the 2026-07-22 deep study (broad, all-area) reconciled with an independent context-layer audit. It routes the loop to the right authority and lets the deterministic doc-lint (scripts/assurance/doc_lint.py) apply staleness rules MODE-AWARE: a WPF/Avalonia mention is a defect in a CURRENT doc but expected in a HISTORICAL one; an absolute host path is a defect in always-loaded guidance but correct in HOST_STATE.md; a pinned wheel hash is a defect in stable guidance but the whole point of a FROZEN_EVIDENCE research record. This registry MIRRORS the doc authorities; it never overrides them (CURRENT_STATE.md still wins on feature state, etc.).

flags.always_loaded: loaded into every session (CLAUDE.md and its @AGENTS.md import); host/volatile facts here are highest-cost drift
flags.stable_guidance: durable agent guidance that must stay host-portable (always_loaded set + the project skill); subject to the host-path and volatile-identity rules

sources.note: Top-level Claude Code contract; @-imports AGENTS.md. Should be a minimal router; currently duplicates host/checkout facts that HOST_STATE.md owns.
Always-loaded agent contract + honesty invariants. Stable, but embeds volatile seq-envelope / exploratory-7B / host-venv detail in the Rules block that belongs in HOST_STATE/HANDOFF.

Compact project router (rewritten from ~318 lines): Tauri 2 + React UI, no absolute host paths, no volatile identities, portability-framed ASCII rule, corrected worker-closure classification (planner is runtime-reachable), research detail pointed to the overlay.
Overview; residual in-progress WPF/Avalonia #545/#546 phrasing and a dataset-centric identity framing vs the seven co-equal areas.
Generated MIT-skeleton file list; still lists removed C# desktop/Avalonia artifacts and omits current files. Needs a mark-HISTORICAL banner, not a rewrite.
Newest-first session log. The correct HOME for volatile ids/hashes/run state; not subject to the volatile-identity rule.

Self-declared single source of truth for feature state (wins on conflict), BUT mixes current + migration history + removed desktop + future work. Repair via section-state marking (CURRENT/HISTORICAL/REMOVED/MIGRATION_COMPLETE/FUTURE), not erasure.

Canonical for host/env AND the correct home for absolute host paths; structured tables trail (kernel 6.8.0-134 vs live -136; env tables stop at v7 while prose carries v8/v9). v1-v8 sealed lineage sections are FROZEN within it.
